name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: We owe it to migrant children and families to reach an immediate solution. We owe it to our law enforcement to prevent this difficult situation from continuing.   We must come together, and we must take action. Now.

name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: Once we’ve taken the necessary steps to ensure these children are in safe and sanitary conditions, then we must take up the long-overdue task of reforming our long-term immigration policy.

name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: The evidence is clear: conditions at these facilities have not improved, and until they do, I will not remove my holds on this Administration’s nominees.

name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: I refuse to stand by while this takes place on American soil. So I took action by placing holds on two individuals nominated to serve in administrative and policy roles at DHS until conditions in these facilities drastically improve.

name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: We saw children stuffed into crowded spaces. The people detained in these facilities lack access to basic necessities like toothpaste and access to sanitary supplies. There are few, if any, pediatricians. No child welfare professionals. No hope.

name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: Families are still being separated. Traumatized children are still in cages, and this Administration continues to ignore basic human rights. Children should never be held in these conditions under any circumstance, for any amount of time. Period.

name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: Children and their families are coming to our country for the same reason so many of our ancestors did – because they have no other choice. We are a nation of immigrants built on a foundation of core values, and we do not turn away those fleeing persecution.

name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: Make no mistake about it: there is a humanitarian crisis at our southern border, and we are failing to address it.   This Administration is failing to address it. This Congress is failing to address it.  https://t.co/XsoH3HdbAP

name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: I’m about to take to the Senate floor at 11:30AM ET/8:30AM PT to share what I saw at the border last week and what we need to begin to address this humanitarian crisis.

name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: Last month, I placed holds on two DHS nominees until conditions in facilities like McAllen drastically improve to meet the standards DHS is obligated to uphold.

name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: We need child welfare professionals in these facilities, and we need more pediatricians and medical professionals. The situation is dire.

name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: I heard from families who have no idea when they’ll be released. They’re scared. Some have open cases in immigration court, and some don’t. Our system is failing these families.

name: Senator Jacky Rosen

tweet: These children are not criminals. They're fleeing unimaginable violence in their home countries and looking for safety.

name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: At CBP, we saw immigrant families held in what looked very much like jail cells. Unaccompanied children are held in a bare, open space for days. This is where they eat, sleep, and wait to hear where they will be sent next. They are anxious and hopeless.

name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: In El Paso, I also toured a Customs and Border Protection processing center and holding facility where families seeking asylum and unaccompanied migrant children are held.

name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: The tent city looks more like a military installation in a foreign country than a place where children are cared for in the U.S.

name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: These children are being held in dusty tents. They are in an unfamiliar place and far from their families and friends. Their showers and bathrooms are outside in trailers. They’re given damp rags to protect their faces from the heat and dust.

name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: Seeing these minors alone without any idea what will happen next was disturbing. No child should have to live in these conditions.

name: Senator Jacky Rosen
tweet: This week, I went to Texas and toured the Tornillo detention facility outside of El Paso. This is the site of the tent city holding unaccompanied immigrant children and children who have been separated from their parents.
